mechanism_narrative: Positive retail sales momentum raises near-term revenue expectations for consumer-facing firms and broad earnings revisions, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days when RSAFS growth is rising.
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS latest available value was 504440 as of two months before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if the sign of 3-month RSAFS growth does not produce a positive 20-day return spread, or if positive RSAFS momentum has lower mean 20-day returns than negative momentum in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: A federal funds rate crossing above a restrictive threshold raises equity discount rates faster than cash-flow expectations adjust, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:FEDFUNDS latest available value was 2.4 as of one month before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations generate positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases or if the triggered-minus-untriggered mean return difference is not negative.

mechanism_narrative: A negative break in industrial production momentum signals weaker real output and lower operating leverage, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:INDPRO latest available value was 109.6678 as of one month before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if negative INDPRO 3-month momentum is followed by nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or if the signal Rank IC is below 0.03 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll deceleration combined with a rising unemployment rate identifies deterioration in labor income and household spending capacity, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change falls below its 12-month average while UNRATE rises over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:PAYEMS latest available value was 150606 and UNRATE was 3.8 as of one month before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-deceleration and unemployment-rise trigger does not underperform single-series labor signals, or if triggered 20-day returns are positive in more than 60% of instances.

mechanism_narrative: Rising housing starts indicate stronger construction activity and durable-goods demand, improving cyclical earnings expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:HOUST latest available value was 1230 as of two months before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 6-month HOUST momentum is not associated with higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative HOUST momentum in walk-forward evaluation.

mechanism_narrative: Low unemployment paired with rising policy rates marks late-cycle Fed restraint, increasing recession-risk premia and discount rates, which produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE crosses below 4.0% while FEDFUNDS 6-month change is positive
evidence_refs: fact:UNRATE was 3.8 and FEDFUNDS was 2.4 in the latest available monthly observations before 2019-03-15
regime_hint: late-cycle monetary tightening with unemployment below 4%
failure_condition: Reject if the joint low-unemployment and rising-FEDFUNDS condition is not followed by negative average 60-day returns or does not underperform periods without the joint condition.

mechanism_narrative: An upside acceleration in CPI inflation raises expected monetary restraint and reduces real equity valuation multiples, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:CPIAUCSL latest available value was 253.113 as of one month before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if CPI acceleration triggers are followed by positive average 5-day S&P 500 returns or if triggered returns are statistically indistinguishable from non-triggered returns at p > 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous weakness in retail sales and housing starts identifies a broad private-demand slowdown that reduces expected earnings growth, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month growth crosses below 0 while HOUST 3-month growth is below 0
evidence_refs: fact:RSAFS and HOUST were the latest available demand indicators before 2019-03-15
failure_condition: Reject if the joint RSAFS-HOUST weakness trigger has nonnegative average 60-day S&P 500 returns or fails to underperform either single-series weakness trigger.
